title: Irregular Verbs - Saber and Salir
description: Learn about irregular verbs 'saber' and 'salir', and how to use them in sentences.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Saber. Good. 'I know' is an exception: sé. How would you say 'he knows' or 'she knows'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Sabe. Good. How would you say 'I don't know'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No sé. How would you say 'I don't know what he wants'? Remember, 'what' is 'qué'.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. If you want to emphasize 'he', you can say 'no sé qué él quiere'. Now, how would you say 'she sees'? Remember 'to see' is 'ver'.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Ella ve. Good. How would you say 'I don't want to invite him, but she does'?

turns.speaker: student
expected_answers: no quiero invitarlo, pero ella sí

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No quiero invitarlo, pero ella sí. Now, 'to leave' or 'to go out' is 'salir'. How would you say 'he leaves'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Sale. Good. How do you say 'I'm going out to eat'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Salgo a comer. Good. How would you say 'I have to do it'? Remember 'to do' is 'hacer'.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Tengo que hacerlo. Now, let's look at verbs with the '-go' ending like 'tengo', 'vengo', 'salgo'. How would you say 'I suppose'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Supongo. Good. How would you say 'I must see her'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Debo verla. Good. Now, how would you say 'I'm going to try to eat later'?

turns.speaker: student
expected_answers: voy a intentar comer más tarde

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Voy a intentar comer más tarde. Excellent. Let's recap these useful verbs: 'quiero', 'voy a', 'tengo que', 'puedo', 'debo'. They allow you to easily add another verb after them.

outro.text: You've learned about irregular verbs like 'saber' and 'salir', and how to use them in sentences.
Try creating your own sentences using these verbs. For example, 'I want to go out' or 'I must know'.
These verbs are essential for everyday conversation. Practice using them to build your confidence.
